input.description: Explore LoRA adapter implementation in unsloth
input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase to understand how LoRA adapters are implemented and how their weights are stored/accessed. I need to understand:

1. Where LoRA adapter logic lives (look for files with "lora", "adapter", "peft" in names)
2. How LoRA A and B matrices are stored - what class/module holds them
3. The structure of lora_config or similar config objects (how rank, alpha, target_modules are stored)
4. How existing adapter saving works (save_pretrained for adapters)
5. What the FastLanguageModel class looks like and what methods it exposes

Please read the actual file contents of the most important adapter-related files and report:
- File paths
- Key classes/methods with signatures  
- How to iterate over LoRA layers and extract A/B weight matrices
- How metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) is accessed
- The pattern for how methods get patched onto model classes

146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304	
305	    if tags is not None:
306	        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
307	        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
308	    else:
309	        tags = ["unsloth",]
310	    pass
311	    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
312	
313	    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
314	        if token is None:
315	            raise RuntimeError(
316	                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
317	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
318	            )
319	        pass
320	
321	        if save_method == "lora":
322	            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
323	        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":
324	            print("Unsloth: Saving 4bit Bitsandbytes model. Please wait...")
325	        pass
326	
327	        # Update model tag
328	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
329	            model, save_directory, token,
330	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
331	            old_username = None, private = private,
332	        )
333	
334	        getattr(model, "original_push_to_hub", model.push_to_hub)\
335	        (
336	            repo_id            = save_directory,
337	            use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
338	            commit_message     = commit_message,
339	            private            = private,
340	            token              = token,
341	            max_shard_size     = max_shard_size,
342	            create_pr          = create_pr,
343	            safe_serialization = safe_serialization,
344	            revision           = revision,
345	            commit_description = commit_description,
346	            tags               = tags,
347	        )
348	        if tokenizer is not None:
349	            # Set padding side to left for inference
350	            old_padding_side = tokenizer.padding_side
351	            tokenizer.padding_side = "left"
352	
353	            getattr(tokenizer, "original_push_to_hub", tokenizer.push_to_hub)\
354	            (
355	                repo_id            = save_directory,
356	                use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
357	                commit_message     = commit_message,
358	                private            = private,
359	                token              = token,
360	                max_shard_size     = max_shard_size,
361	                create_pr          = create_pr,
362	                safe_serialization = safe_serialization,
363	                revision           = revision,
364	                commit_description = commit_description,
365	                tags               = tags,
366	            )
367	
368	            # Revert back padding side
369	            tokenizer.padding_side = old_padding_side
370	        pass
371	
372	        if hasattr(model, "config"):
373	            print(f"Saved {save_method} model to https://huggingface.co/" + save_directory)
374	        pass
375	        return save_directory, None
376	    pass
377	
378	    # Tokenizer has different saving arguments
379	    tokenizer_save_settings = \
380	    {
381	        "save_directory"  : save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"],
382	        "legacy_format"   : None,
383	        "filename_prefix" : None,
384	        "push_to_hub"     : save_pretrained_settings["push_to_hub"],
385	        "private"         : save_pretrained_settings["private"],
386	        "token"           : save_pretrained_settings["token"],
387	    }
388	
389	    # Check if PEFT Model or not - if yes, 3 levels. If not 2 levels.
390	    from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM
391	    if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
392	        internal_model = model.model
393	    else:
394	        internal_model = model
395	    pass
396	
397	    # Cannot be converted properly!
398	    if (save_method == "merged_4bit") or (save_method == "lora") or (
399	        not hasattr(model, "model") or \
400	        not hasattr(internal_model.model, "layers")
401	    ):
402	        # Do general saving
403	        # Edit save_pretrained_settings
404	        # [TODO] _create_repo has errors due to **kwargs getting accepted
405	        # commit_description does not seem to work?
406	        what_to_delete = ("use_temp_dir", "commit_message", "create_pr", "revision", "commit_description", "tags",) \
407	            if save_pretrained_settings["push_to_hub"] is False else \
408	            ("use_temp_dir", "create_pr", "revision", "tags", "commit_description",)
409	        for deletion in what_to_delete:
410	            del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
411	        pass
412	        if hasattr(model, "add_model_tags"):
413	            model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
414	
415	        # Update model tag
416	        if push_to_hub:
417	             _ = upload_to_huggingface(
418	                model, save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"], token,
419	                "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
420	                old_username = None, private = private,
421	            )
422	        pass
423	
424	        if tokenizer is not None:
425	            print("Unsloth: Saving tokenizer...", end = "")
426	
427	            # Set padding side to left for inference
428	            old_padding_side = tokenizer.padding_side
429	            tokenizer.padding_side = "left"
430	
431	            tokenizer.save_pretrained(**tokenizer_save_settings)
432	
433	            # Revert back padding side
434	            tokenizer.padding_side = old_padding_side
435	
436	            print(" Done.")
437	        else:
438	            print()
439	
440	        print("Unsloth: Saving model...", end = "")
441	        if save_method != "lora": print(" This might take 10 minutes for Llama-7b...", end = "")
442	
443	        # [TODO] Is this correct?
444	        if save_method == "lora":
445	            save_pretrained_settings["selected_adapters"] = None
446	        pass
447	
448	        model.save_pretrained(**save_pretrained_settings)
449	
450	        if push_to_hub and hasattr(model, "config"):
451	            print("Saved to https://huggingface.co/" + save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"])
452	        pass
453	
454	        print(" Done.")

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160

162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

192	def LlamaAttention_fast_forward_inference(
193	    self,
194	    hidden_states:  torch.Tensor,
195	    past_key_value: Optional[Tuple[torch.Tensor]],
196	    position_ids,
197	    do_prefill = False,
198	    attention_mask = None,
199	):
200	    """
201	        https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/llama/modeling_llama.py#L406
202	        Fast inference using KV cache.
203	        QK^T can be computed in 4 chunks
204	
205	        [Q, q] @ [K, k].T where q, k are the new tokens.
206	        [QK^T, Qk^T]
207	        [qK^T, qk^T]
208	
209	        Since the attention mask wipes Qk^T, we just get
210	        [QK^T,    0]
211	        [qK^T, qk^T]
212	
213	        Since softmax is row-wise, we get
214	        softmax([QK^T,    0])
215	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T])
216	
217	        We then multiply by   [V]
218	                              [v]
219	        softmax([QK^T,    0]) [softmax(QK^T)V] *
220	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) [softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) @ [V, v]]
221	
222	        But notice * [softmax(QK^T)V] is just the last attention.
223	        We just need to compute the last final row.
224	
225	        This means we can pass in a row of Q, but we need to
226	        remember K and V, which are called the KV cache.
227	    """
228	    Xn = hidden_states
229	    bsz, _, hd = hidden_states.size()
230	    K1, V1 = past_key_value
231	    dtype = Xn.dtype
232	
233	    n_heads    = self.config.num_attention_heads
234	    n_groups   = self.num_key_value_groups
235	    n_kv_heads = self.config.num_key_value_heads
236	    head_dim   = self.head_dim
237	    # assert(n_kv_heads * n_groups == n_heads)
238	
239	    hidden_size = self.config.hidden_size
240	    attention_size = n_heads*head_dim
241	    seq_len = K1.shape[-2]
242	    kv_seq_len = seq_len + 1
243	
244	    # Prefill phase
245	    # if not hasattr(self, "paged_attention"):
246	    device = hidden_states.device
247	    if do_prefill:
248	        self.paged_attention = torch.empty((KV_CACHE_INCREMENT+seq_len+1, 2, bsz, n_kv_heads, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
249	        self.paged_attention_K = self.paged_attention[:,0]
250	        self.paged_attention_V = self.paged_attention[:,1]
251	        self.paged_attention_K[:seq_len] = K1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
252	        self.paged_attention_V[:seq_len] = V1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
253	        self.temp_QA = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, attention_size), dtype = dtype, device = device)
254	        self.temp_KV = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, n_kv_heads*head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
255	        self.RH_Q = torch.empty((bsz, n_heads, 1, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
256	
257	        # Mistral Nemo 12b has weird dimensions
258	        if attention_size != hidden_size:
259	            self.temp_O = torch.empty((1, bsz, hidden_size), dtype = dtype, device = device)
260	        else:
261	            self.temp_O = self.temp_QA[1][:,:,:hidden_size]
262	        pass
263	
264	        self.attention = torch.empty((bsz, n_heads, 1, KV_CACHE_INCREMENT+seq_len), dtype = dtype, device = device)
265	        self.scalar = 1.0 / math_sqrt(self.head_dim)
266	        self.half_head_dim = head_dim // 2
267	    elif kv_seq_len >= self.paged_attention.shape[0]:
268	        self.paged_attention.resize_((self.paged_attention.shape[0]+KV_CACHE_INCREMENT, 2, bsz, n_kv_heads, head_dim))
269	        self.paged_attention_K = self.paged_attention[:,0]
270	        self.paged_attention_V = self.paged_attention[:,1]
271	        self.attention.resize_((bsz, n_heads, 1, self.attention.shape[-1]+KV_CACHE_INCREMENT))
272	    pass
273	
274	    Qn = fast_linear_forward(self.q_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_QA[0])
275	    Kn = fast_linear_forward(self.k_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_KV[0])
276	    Vn = fast_linear_forward(self.v_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_KV[1])
277	    Qn = Qn.view(bsz, 1, n_heads,    head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
278	    Kn = Kn.view(bsz, 1, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
279	    Vn = Vn.view(bsz, 1, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
280	
281	    # cos, sin = self.rotary_emb(Vn, seq_len = kv_seq_len)
282	    # Qn, Kn = inplace_rope_embedding(Qn, Kn, cos, sin, position_ids)
283	
284	    # Need to do it prior 2 steps before hitting full on short KV cache
285	    # or else error
286	    self.rotary_emb.extend_rope_embedding(Vn, seq_len + 2)
287	    cos, sin = self.rotary_emb.get_cached(kv_seq_len, Qn.device.index)
288	    cos = cos[position_ids].unsqueeze(1)
289	    sin = sin[position_ids].unsqueeze(1)
290	    h = self.half_head_dim
291	
292	    RH_Q = self.RH_Q
293	    RH_Q[:,:,:,:h] = Qn[:,:,:,h:]
294	    RH_Q[:,:,:,h:] = Qn[:,:,:,:h]
295	    RH_Q[:,:,:,:h].neg_() # torch.neg(RH_Q[:,:,:,:h], out = RH_Q[:,:,:,:h])
296	    Qn *= cos
297	    Qn.addcmul_(RH_Q, sin)
298

2956	        def _for_inference(m):

2963	        pass
2964	        m = model
2965	        while hasattr(m, "model"):

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the key information. Let me create a comprehensive report of my findings.

## Summary of LoRA Adapter Implementation in Unsloth

Based on my exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed understanding of how LoRA adapters are implemented:

### 1. **File Locations for LoRA Logic**

**Core LoRA Files:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Custom autograd functions for LoRA forward/backward passes
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - LoRA parameter extraction utilities (get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias)
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Main model class (FastLlamaModel) with get_peft_model() and patch_peft_model() methods
- `/unsloth/models/loader.py` - FastLanguageModel class entry point
- `/unsloth/save.py` - Model saving logic including _merge_lora() function
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - Utility functions for model patching

### 2. **LoRA A and B Matrices Storage**

**How they're stored:**
LoRA adapters are **PEFT-based** and stored as attributes on Linear layers that have been wrapped by PEFT:

```python
# From kernels/utils.py:190-231 (get_lora_parameters function)
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA weights from PEFT wrappers
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight      # Shape: (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight      # Shape: (out_features, r)
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Data Structure:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA A weights (reduces input to rank r)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA B weights (expands rank r to output)
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scaling factor (lora_alpha / r)

**Where found:**
- Individual projection layers: `q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`, `gate_proj`, `up_proj`, `down_proj`
- Accessed via: `model.model.layers[idx].<layer_name>.lora_A[adapter_name].weight`

### 3. **LoRA Config Storage**

**Config Object Structure** (from `models/llama.py:2598-2617`):

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                    # Rank (default 16)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,           # Scaling factor (default 16)
    target_modules      = final_modules,        # Which layers to apply LoRA
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,         # Dropout (default 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                 # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,   # Task type
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,  # Which layers to target (None = all)
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,    # How to initialize weights
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,         # LoftQ init config
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,           # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,      # Modules to fully train (e.g., lm_head)
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

**Access pattern:**
```python
# From model after patching:
model.peft_config['default'].r                  # Get rank
model.peft_config['default'].lora_alpha         # Get alpha
model.peft_config['default'].target_modules     # Get target modules
model.peft_config['default'].to_dict()          # Get full config
```

### 4. **Adapter Saving Implementation**

**Save Methods:**

a) **LoRA-only saving** (`save_method="lora"`):
```python
# models/llama.py:2649
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```
Calls PEFT's `save_pretrained()` which saves:
- `adapter_config.json` - LoRA config (r, alpha, target_modules, etc.)
- `adapter_model.bin` - Only the LoRA A/B weights

b) **Merged saving** (`save_method="merged_16bit"`):
```python
# save.py:159-187 (_merge_lora function)
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    base_layer = getattr(layer, "base_layer", layer)
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    else:
        dtype = W.dtype
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

Formula: `W_merged = W_original + (s * A^T @ B^T)` where `s = lora_alpha / r`

**Save Process** (save.py:205-456):
1. Detects if model is PeftModel
2. For LoRA: calls `model.save_pretrained()` (delegated to PEFT)
3. For merged: iterates layers, calls `_merge_lora()` on each, builds merged state_dict

### 5. **FastLanguageModel Class**

**Main Entry Point** (`models/loader.py:107`):
```python
class FastLanguageModel(FastLlamaModel):
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(
        model_name = "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
        max_seq_length = 2048,
        load_in_4bit = True,
        full_finetuning = False,
        # ... 30+ parameters
    ):
        # Routes to FastModel or FastLlamaModel based on loading strategy
```

**Key Methods:**

1. **get_peft_model()** (models/llama.py:2266-2665):
   - Creates LoraConfig with specified parameters
   - Calls PEFT's `_get_peft_model(model, lora_config)`
   - Applies custom Unsloth optimizations via `patch_peft_model()`

2. **patch_peft_model()** (models/llama.py:2732-2947):
   - Patches attention layers to use custom `apply_lora_qkv()` function
   - Patches MLP layers to use custom `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` function
   - Patches output projection to use custom `apply_lora_o()` function
   - Replaces default PEFT forward passes with optimized Unsloth kernels

3. **from_pretrained()** (models/llama.py:1815-2100+):
   - Loads base model from HF
   - Detects if it's a PEFT adapter or base model
   - Routes appropriately

**Exposed Methods:**
```python
# Training setup
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)

# Inference setup
model.for_inference()

# Training
trainer = SFTTrainer(model=model, ...)

# Saving
model.save_pretrained("my-lora", selected_adapters=["default"])
```

### 6. **Iterating Over LoRA Layers and Extracting A/B Matrices**

**Pattern to iterate and extract:**

```python
# Access all LoRA layers
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0]  # or model.peft_config['default']
rank = model.peft_config[active_adapter].r
alpha = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_alpha

# Iterate through layers
for layer_idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Attention projections
    for proj_name in ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"]:
        proj = getattr(layer.self_attn, proj_name)
        if hasattr(proj, "lora_A"):
            A = proj.lora_A[active_adapter].weight     # Shape: (rank, in_features)
            B = proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight     # Shape: (out_features, rank)
            scaling = proj.scaling[active_adapter]     # Alpha / Rank
    
    # MLP projections
    for proj_name in ["gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]:
        proj = getattr(layer.mlp, proj_name)
        if hasattr(proj, "lora_A"):
            A = proj.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
            B = proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
            scaling = proj.scaling[active_adapter]
```

**Using get_lora_parameters utility:**
```python
from unsloth.kernels.utils import get_lora_parameters

# Returns 5-tuple: (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)
W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(proj)

if A is not None:  # Adapter is active
    # A: (rank, in_features)
    # B: (out_features, rank)
    # Computation: output = base_output + scaling * (input @ A.T @ B.T)
```

### 7. **Method Patching Pattern**

**How methods get patched onto model classes:**

```python
# models/llama.py:2845-2887
import types

# For MLP layers
mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)

# For attention QKV
layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv

# For output projection
layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o

# For model class methods
model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model
)
```

**Patching mechanism:**
1. `patch_saving_functions(model)` - replaces save/push methods
2. `patch_peft_model(model)` - replaces forward methods with optimized versions
3. Uses `types.MethodType()` to dynamically bind functions to instances

### 8. **Key Metadata Access**

```python
# Configuration access
config = model.peft_config['default']
rank = config.r
alpha = config.lora_alpha
target_modules = config.target_modules
bias = config.bias
lora_dropout = config.lora_dropout

# Active adapter
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0]

# Check if layer has LoRA
has_lora = hasattr(layer.self_attn.q_proj, "lora_A")
is_merged = model.peft_config[active_adapter].merged

# Disable/enable adapters
model.disable_adapters_()
model.enable_adapters_()
```

### 9. **LoRA Forward Pass (from kernels/fast_lora.py)**

The custom forward functions show the computation pattern:

```python
# Attention: Q = base_Q + scaling * (input @ A.Q @ B.Q)
Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)

# MLP: output = base_output + sum of LoRA contributions
out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS, ...)
```

**File Paths Summary:**

| Component | File |
|-----------|------|
| LoRA custom autograd | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| LoRA parameter extraction | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (get_lora_parameters) |
| Model class definition | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (FastLlamaModel) |
| Entry point | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (FastLanguageModel) |
| Save/merge logic | `/unsloth/save.py` (_merge_lora, unsloth_save_model) |
| Method patching | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (patch_peft_model) |

This architecture allows Unsloth to leverage PEFT's LoRA config/structure while providing custom optimized kernels for training and inference.

```python
    else:
        dtype = W.dtype
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

Formula: `W_merged = W_original + (s * A^T @ B^T)` where `s = lora_alpha / r`

**Save Process** (save.py:205-456):
1. Detects if model is PeftModel
2. For LoRA: calls `model.save_pretrained()` (delegated to PEFT)
3. For merged: iterates layers, calls `_merge_lora()` on each, builds merged state_dict

### 5. **FastLanguageModel Class**

**Main Entry Point** (`models/loader.py:107`):
```python
class FastLanguageModel(FastLlamaModel):
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(
        model_name = "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
        max_seq_length = 2048,
        load_in_4bit = True,
        full_finetuning = False,
        # ... 30+ parameters
    ):
        # Routes to FastModel or FastLlamaModel based on loading strategy
```

**Key Methods:**

1. **get_peft_model()** (models/llama.py:2266-2665):
   - Creates LoraConfig with specified parameters
   - Calls PEFT's `_get_peft_model(model, lora_config)`
   - Applies custom Unsloth optimizations via `patch_peft_model()`

2. **patch_peft_model()** (models/llama.py:2732-2947):
   - Patches attention layers to use custom `apply_lora_qkv()` function
   - Patches MLP layers to use custom `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` function
   - Patches output projection to use custom `apply_lora_o()` function
   - Replaces default PEFT forward passes with optimized Unsloth kernels

3. **from_pretrained()** (models/llama.py:1815-2100+):
   - Loads base model from HF
   - Detects if it's a PEFT adapter or base model
   - Routes appropriately

**Exposed Methods:**
```python
# Training setup
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)

# Inference setup
model.for_inference()

# Training
trainer = SFTTrainer(model=model, ...)

# Saving
model.save_pretrained("my-lora", selected_adapters=["default"])
```

### 6. **Iterating Over LoRA Layers and Extracting A/B Matrices**

**Pattern to iterate and extract:**

```python
# Access all LoRA layers
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0]  # or model.peft_config['default']
rank = model.peft_config[active_adapter].r
alpha = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_alpha

# Iterate through layers
for layer_idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Attention projections
    for proj_name in ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"]:
        proj = getattr(layer.self_attn, proj_name)
        if hasattr(proj, "lora_A"):
            A = proj.lora_A[active_adapter].weight     # Shape: (rank, in_features)
            B = proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight     # Shape: (out_features, rank)
            scaling = proj.scaling[active_adapter]     # Alpha / Rank
    
    # MLP projections
    for proj_name in ["gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]:
        proj = getattr(layer.mlp, proj_name)
        if hasattr(proj, "lora_A"):
            A = proj.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
            B = proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
            scaling = proj.scaling[active_adapter]
```

**Using get_lora_parameters utility:**
```python
from unsloth.kernels.utils import get_lora_parameters

# Returns 5-tuple: (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)
W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(proj)

if A is not None:  # Adapter is active
    # A: (rank, in_features)
    # B: (out_features, rank)
    # Computation: output = base_output + scaling * (input @ A.T @ B.T)
```

### 7. **Method Patching Pattern**

**How methods get patched onto model classes:**

```python
# models/llama.py:2845-2887
import types

# For MLP layers
mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)

# For attention QKV
layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv

# For output projection
layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o

# For model class methods
model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model
)
```

**Patching mechanism:**
1. `patch_saving_functions(model)` - replaces save/push methods
2. `patch_peft_model(model)` - replaces forward methods with optimized versions
3. Uses `types.MethodType()` to dynamically bind functions to instances

### 8. **Key Metadata Access**

```python
# Configuration access
config = model.peft_config['default']
rank = config.r
alpha = config.lora_alpha
target_modules = config.target_modules
bias = config.bias
lora_dropout = config.lora_dropout

# Active adapter
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0]

# Check if layer has LoRA
has_lora = hasattr(layer.self_attn.q_proj, "lora_A")
is_merged = model.peft_config[active_adapter].merged

# Disable/enable adapters
model.disable_adapters_()
model.enable_adapters_()
```

### 9. **LoRA Forward Pass (from kernels/fast_lora.py)**

The custom forward functions show the computation pattern:

```python
# Attention: Q = base_Q + scaling * (input @ A.Q @ B.Q)
Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)

# MLP: output = base_output + sum of LoRA contributions
out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS, ...)
```

**File Paths Summary:**

| Component | File |
|-----------|------|
| LoRA custom autograd | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| LoRA parameter extraction | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (get_lora_parameters) |
| Model class definition | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (FastLlamaModel) |
| Entry point | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (FastLanguageModel) |
| Save/merge logic | `/unsloth/save.py` (_merge_lora, unsloth_save_model) |
| Method patching | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (patch_peft_model) |

This architecture allows Unsloth to leverage PEFT's LoRA config/structure while providing custom optimized kernels for training and inference.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Save/Export Implementation

I've thoroughly explored the unsloth codebase to understand its structure and save/export functionality. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. Overall Directory Structure

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main entry point, device configuration
├── save.py                        # Core saving/export implementation (2678 lines)
├── chat_templates.py              # Chat template utilities (includes patching)
├── tokenizer_utils.py             # Tokenizer utilities including GGUF fixes
├── trainer.py                     # Training utilities
├── models/                        # Model-specific implementations
│   ├── llama.py                   # Llama model implementation (2900+ lines)
│   ├── mistral.py                 # Mistral model support
│   ├── qwen3.py, qwen2.py         # Qwen models
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py        # Gemma models
│   ├── vision.py                  # Vision model support
│   ├── loader.py                  # Model loading logic
│   ├── mapper.py                  # Weight mapping utilities
│   ├── _utils.py                  # General utilities
│   └── rl.py                      # Reinforcement learning models
├── kernels/                       # Cuda/Triton optimization kernels
│   ├── utils.py                   # Contains get_lora_parameters_bias()
│   ├── fast_lora.py               # Fast LoRA computation
│   └── __init__.py                # Exports QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
├── registry/                      # Model registry system
│   ├── registry.py                # Registry management
│   ├── _llama.py, _mistral.py, etc # Model registrations
│   └── REGISTRY.md                # Registry documentation
└── dataprep/                      # Data preparation utilities
```

### 2. Key Files for Save/Export Functionality

**File: `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)**

This is the primary file containing all save/export logic.

**Key Functions:**

1. **`unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method="lora", ...)`** (lines 205-759)
   - **Purpose:** Main entry point for saving models in 3 modes: "lora", "merged_16bit", or "merged_4bit"
   - **Key logic:**
     - Validates save_method
     - For "lora": Calls PEFT's standard save_pretrained with selected_adapters=None
     - For "merged_*": Merges LoRA weights into base model weights and saves
   - **Returns:** (save_directory, None)

2. **`_merge_lora(layer, name)`** (lines 159-187)
   - **Purpose:** Core LoRA merging logic
   - **Implementation:**
     ```python
     W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
     if quant_state is not None:
         W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
     W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
     if A is not None:
         W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
     W = W.t().to(dtype)
     ```
   - **Key Parameters:** Accesses lora_A[adapter].weight, lora_B[adapter].weight, and scaling[adapter]

3. **`save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, ...)`** (lines 952-1320)
   - **Purpose:** Converts 4-bit quantized model to GGUF format using llama.cpp
   - **Process:**
     1. Installs llama.cpp if not present
     2. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit (via convert-hf-to-gguf.py)
     3. Applies quantization methods (q4_k_m, q8_0, q5_k_m, f16, etc.)
   - **Supports:** Multiple quantization methods per call

4. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)`** (lines 1768-1950)
   - **Purpose:** Method attached to model for saving to GGUF
   - **Process:**
     1. Fixes tokenizer BOS token
     2. Installs llama.cpp non-blocking
     3. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with save_method="merged_16bit"
     4. Converts merged model to GGUF via save_to_gguf()
   - **Quantization methods:** f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q2_k, q3_k_*, etc.

5. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, save_directory, tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit", ...)`** (lines 1321-1361)
   - **Purpose:** Method for saving merged models (wrapper around unsloth_save_model)
   - **Delegates to:** unsloth_save_model() with provided arguments

6. **`patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`** (lines 2569-2677)
   - **Purpose:** Dynamically attaches save/push methods to model instances
   - **Registered methods:**
     ```python
     model.save_pretrained_merged     # Save merged model (16-bit or 4-bit)
     model.push_to_hub_merged         # Push merged model to HuggingFace
     model.save_pretrained_gguf       # Save as GGUF
     model.push_to_hub_gguf           # Push GGUF to HuggingFace
     model.save_pretrained_ggml       # Convert LoRA to GGML
     model.push_to_hub_ggml           # Push GGML to HuggingFace
     model.save_pretrained_torchao    # Save with TorchAO quantization
     model.push_to_hub (patched)      # Enhanced push_to_hub with Unsloth tags
     ```
   - **Implementation:** Uses `types.MethodType` to bind functions as methods

7. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(self, tokenizer, repo_id, ...)`** (lines 2143-2209)
   - **Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - **Process:**
     1. Saves LoRA adapters locally
     2. Runs llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py
     3. Uploads resulting ggml-adapter-model.bin to HuggingFace

8. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config=None, ...)`** (lines 2502-2561)
   - **Purpose:** Saves model with TorchAO quantization
   - **Process:**
     1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
     2. Loads merged model
     3. Applies TorchAO quantization
     4. Saves or pushes to hub

### 3. LoRA Adapter Weight Access Pattern

**File: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)**

**Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`**

Returns a 6-tuple:
```python
(W, quant_state, lora_A.weight, lora_B.weight, scaling, bias)
```

**Key implementation details:**
```python
# Get active adapter name
adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter is None: 
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
adapter = adapter[0]

# Access the actual weight matrices
lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict-like access by adapter name
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
A = lora_A_linear.weight                  # Extract weight tensor
B = lora_B_linear.weight
s = proj.scaling[adapter]                 # Scaling factor

# Optional fake quantization for QAT
lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
    A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
    B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
```

**Key data structures accessed:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to Linear layers containing A matrices
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to Linear layers containing B matrices
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to scaling factors (typically lora_alpha / lora_rank)
- `proj.base_layer.weight` - Base model weight (may be quantized)
- `proj.disable_adapters` - Flag to check if adapters are disabled

### 4. Merged Model Saving Implementation

**Location:** Lines 483-759 in save.py

**Key steps:**

1. **State dict creation:** Creates OrderedDict to hold merged weights
2. **Layer-by-layer merging:**
   ```python
   for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
       for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
           proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
           W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)
           state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = W
           if bias is not None:
               state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.bias"] = bias
   ```
3. **Embedding and norm layers:** Handles embed_tokens, norm weights separately
4. **Memory management:** Uses temporary disk storage if RAM insufficient
5. **Config cleanup:** Removes quantization_config from saved model config
6. **Tokenizer saving:** Saves with padding_side="left" for inference

### 5. GGUF Export Implementation

**Key GGUF-related functions:**

1. **save_to_gguf()** (lines 952-1320)
   - Installs llama.cpp (using make or cmake)
   - Validates quantization methods against ALLOWED_QUANTS dictionary
   - Executes: `python convert-hf-to-gguf.py` then `llama-quantize`
   - Creates multiple quantized versions in one call

2. **Quantization methods supported:**
   ```python
   ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
       "f32": "Full precision",
       "f16": "Float16 - fastest conversion",
       "bf16": "Bfloat16 - fastest conversion",
       "q8_0": "8-bit quantization",
       "q4_k_m": "4-bit with mixed precision (recommended)",
       "q5_k_m": "5-bit with mixed precision (recommended)",
       "q2_k", "q3_k_s", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_l": "3-bit variants",
       "not_quantized": "Maps to f16",
       "fast_quantized": "Maps to q8_0",
       "quantized": "Maps to q4_k_m"
   }
   ```

### 6. Method Registration Pattern

**Pattern used in patch_saving_functions() (lines 2569-2677):**

```python
# 1. Create function with exec() and signature matching original
exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())  # Create unsloth_push_to_hub dynamically

# 2. Bind to model instances using types.MethodType
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, 
    model
)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, 
    model
)
# ... and so on for other save methods
```

**Key property:** Methods are bound after `patch_saving_functions()` is called during model loading (in `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`)

### 7. Metadata Handling

**LoRA metadata is preserved via PEFT:**
- When saving with save_method="lora": Uses PEFT's standard adapter_config.json format
- Metadata automatically saved by PEFT includes:
  - lora_rank (r)
  - lora_alpha (scaling factor)
  - target_modules (which layers have adapters)
  - lora_dropout
  - bias configuration

**Quantization metadata:**
- For 4-bit models: quantization_config saved in config.json
- For merged 16-bit: quantization_config is explicitly removed before saving
- For GGUF: Metadata handled by llama.cpp (model type, vocab, etc.)

### 8. Main Entry Points

**For end users:**
```python
# Via FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)

# Save as LoRA
model.save_pretrained_merged(save_dir, tokenizer, save_method="lora")

# Save as merged 16-bit (for GGUF)
model.save_pretrained_merged(save_dir, tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")

# Save as GGUF
model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_dir, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")

# Convert LoRA to GGML
model.save_pretrained_ggml(save_dir, tokenizer)

# Push to HuggingFace
model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")
model.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")
```

**Internal flow:**
1. Model loading calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` in models/llama.py line 2204
2. This dynamically attaches all save methods to the model instance
3. Methods delegate to core functions in save.py

### 9. Key Classes/Weights Structure

**Weight layer structure (from LLAMA_WEIGHTS constant, line 67-70):**
```python
"self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
"mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj"
```

**Norm layers (line 71-75):**
```python
"input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
"pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
"self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm"
```

**Module classes supporting LoRA:**
- `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` - 4-bit quantized linear layers
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` - PEFT's 4-bit wrapper
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` - PEFT's regular linear wrapper

### 10. Test Coverage

**Test file: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`**

Tests validate:
- `save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_16bit")`
- `save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_4bit_forced")`
- Model loading from saved paths
- File structure and tokenizer presence
- Quantization config presence/absence

---

This comprehensive exploration provides a complete picture of how unsloth implements its save/export functionality with detailed understanding of LoRA weight access, metadata handling, and the pattern used to expose save methods to end users.

     ```python
     model.push_to_hub_ggml           # Push GGML to HuggingFace
     model.save_pretrained_torchao    # Save with TorchAO quantization
     model.push_to_hub (patched)      # Enhanced push_to_hub with Unsloth tags
     ```
   - **Implementation:** Uses `types.MethodType` to bind functions as methods

7. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(self, tokenizer, repo_id, ...)`** (lines 2143-2209)
   - **Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - **Process:**
     1. Saves LoRA adapters locally
     2. Runs llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py
     3. Uploads resulting ggml-adapter-model.bin to HuggingFace

8. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config=None, ...)`** (lines 2502-2561)
   - **Purpose:** Saves model with TorchAO quantization
   - **Process:**
     1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
     2. Loads merged model
     3. Applies TorchAO quantization
     4. Saves or pushes to hub

### 3. LoRA Adapter Weight Access Pattern

**File: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)**

**Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`**

Returns a 6-tuple:
```python
(W, quant_state, lora_A.weight, lora_B.weight, scaling, bias)
```

**Key implementation details:**
```python
# Get active adapter name
adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter is None: 
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
adapter = adapter[0]

# Access the actual weight matrices
lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict-like access by adapter name
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
A = lora_A_linear.weight                  # Extract weight tensor
B = lora_B_linear.weight
s = proj.scaling[adapter]                 # Scaling factor

# Optional fake quantization for QAT
lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
    A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
    B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
```

**Key data structures accessed:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to Linear layers containing A matrices
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to Linear layers containing B matrices
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter names to scaling factors (typically lora_alpha / lora_rank)
- `proj.base_layer.weight` - Base model weight (may be quantized)
- `proj.disable_adapters` - Flag to check if adapters are disabled

### 4. Merged Model Saving Implementation

**Location:** Lines 483-759 in save.py

**Key steps:**

1. **State dict creation:** Creates OrderedDict to hold merged weights
2. **Layer-by-layer merging:**
   ```python
   for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
       for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
           proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
           W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)
           state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = W
           if bias is not None:
               state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.bias"] = bias
   ```
3. **Embedding and norm layers:** Handles embed_tokens, norm weights separately
4. **Memory management:** Uses temporary disk storage if RAM insufficient
5. **Config cleanup:** Removes quantization_config from saved model config
6. **Tokenizer saving:** Saves with padding_side="left" for inference

### 5. GGUF Export Implementation

**Key GGUF-related functions:**

1. **save_to_gguf()** (lines 952-1320)
   - Installs llama.cpp (using make or cmake)
   - Validates quantization methods against ALLOWED_QUANTS dictionary
   - Executes: `python convert-hf-to-gguf.py` then `llama-quantize`
   - Creates multiple quantized versions in one call

2. **Quantization methods supported:**
   ```python
   ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
       "f32": "Full precision",
       "f16": "Float16 - fastest conversion",
       "bf16": "Bfloat16 - fastest conversion",
       "q8_0": "8-bit quantization",
       "q4_k_m": "4-bit with mixed precision (recommended)",
       "q5_k_m": "5-bit with mixed precision (recommended)",
       "q2_k", "q3_k_s", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_l": "3-bit variants",
       "not_quantized": "Maps to f16",
       "fast_quantized": "Maps to q8_0",
       "quantized": "Maps to q4_k_m"
   }
   ```

### 6. Method Registration Pattern

**Pattern used in patch_saving_functions() (lines 2569-2677):**

```python
# 1. Create function with exec() and signature matching original
exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())  # Create unsloth_push_to_hub dynamically

# 2. Bind to model instances using types.MethodType
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, 
    model
)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, 
    model
)
# ... and so on for other save methods
```

**Key property:** Methods are bound after `patch_saving_functions()` is called during model loading (in `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`)

### 7. Metadata Handling

**LoRA metadata is preserved via PEFT:**
- When saving with save_method="lora": Uses PEFT's standard adapter_config.json format
- Metadata automatically saved by PEFT includes:
  - lora_rank (r)
  - lora_alpha (scaling factor)
  - target_modules (which layers have adapters)
  - lora_dropout
  - bias configuration

**Quantization metadata:**
- For 4-bit models: quantization_config saved in config.json
- For merged 16-bit: quantization_config is explicitly removed before saving
- For GGUF: Metadata handled by llama.cpp (model type, vocab, etc.)

### 8. Main Entry Points

**For end users:**
```python
# Via FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)

# Save as LoRA
model.save_pretrained_merged(save_dir, tokenizer, save_method="lora")

# Save as merged 16-bit (for GGUF)
model.save_pretrained_merged(save_dir, tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")

# Save as GGUF
model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_dir, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")

# Convert LoRA to GGML
model.save_pretrained_ggml(save_dir, tokenizer)

# Push to HuggingFace
model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")
model.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")
```

**Internal flow:**
1. Model loading calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` in models/llama.py line 2204
2. This dynamically attaches all save methods to the model instance
3. Methods delegate to core functions in save.py

### 9. Key Classes/Weights Structure

**Weight layer structure (from LLAMA_WEIGHTS constant, line 67-70):**
```python
"self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
"mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj"
```

**Norm layers (line 71-75):**
```python
"input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
"pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
"self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm"
```

**Module classes supporting LoRA:**
- `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` - 4-bit quantized linear layers
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` - PEFT's 4-bit wrapper
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` - PEFT's regular linear wrapper

### 10. Test Coverage

**Test file: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`**

Tests validate:
- `save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_16bit")`
- `save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_4bit_forced")`
- Model loading from saved paths
- File structure and tokenizer presence
- Quantization config presence/absence

---

This comprehensive exploration provides a complete picture of how unsloth implements its save/export functionality with detailed understanding of LoRA weight access, metadata handling, and the pattern used to expose save methods to end users.

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass


@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass
